title: Fixing CSS Inline Block Height Calculation

statement.markdown: You are given a CSS string that defines a block of HTML elements. The CSS string consists of multiple inline styles, where each style defines the height of an element. Your task is to calculate the total height of all elements and return the result as a string in the format 'height: <total_height>px'. However, the given CSS string has a logical bug that causes incorrect height calculations. Fix the bug to ensure accurate calculations.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a CSS style definition with multiple inline styles, each defining the height of an element.
statement.outputFormat: A string representing the total height of all elements in the format 'height: <total_height>px'.
statement.notes: The input CSS string will contain multiple styles with the format 'style='height: <height_value>px''

constraints.description: Maximum number of styles in the input CSS string

hints.text: Split the input CSS string into individual styles

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing CSS Inline Block Height Calculation
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to correctly split the input CSS string into individual styles and then calculate the total height by summing up the heights of all elements.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to use regular expressions to split the input CSS string into individual styles. However, this approach may not be efficient for large input strings.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a stack data structure to parse the input CSS string and keep track of the total height.
editorial.optimalSolution: Push each height value onto the stack and then pop all values from the stack to calculate the total height.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution ensures that the total height is calculated correctly by summing up all height values.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly splitting the input CSS string
Failing to sum up all height values
